stem: A triangle has angle measures of 30°, 60°, and 90° and a perimeter of (9 + 3√3) units. What is the length of the longest side of the triangle?
rationale: In a 30°-60°-90° triangle, the side lengths are in ratio 1 : √3 : 2 (short leg : long leg : hypotenuse). Let the short leg be x. Then perimeter = x + x√3 + 2x = (3 + √3)x. Setting (3 + √3)x = 9 + 3√3 = 3(3 + √3), we get x = 3. The hypotenuse (longest side) is 2x = 6.
